campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed housing security concerns and noted that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Housing security issues
Boogie Festival this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 20, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council adopted a resolution stating the city's intention to identify certain properties and scheduled a public hearing for June 17, 2026, where residents can voice concerns and the council will make a final decision on the matter.
keyTopics: Resolution adopted to identify designated properties
Public hearing scheduled for June 17, 2026
Residents can submit protests and comments before final approval

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council held a meeting on June 2, 2026, where they approved previous meeting minutes, selected a youth commissioner, and set up a municipal election for November 3, 2026. The council also authorized various agreements and contracts for town services, including software systems and building permit reviews.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 municipal election scheduled
New youth commissioner appointment approved
Software agreement with Accela, Inc. for five-year term
Final map approved for Tract 10675 development
Building division plan check agreements authorized

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 10, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and plan two upcoming council sessions: one scheduled for June 16, 2026, and another for June 23, 2026. The council reviewed what items should be added to each agenda, decided which topics need specific time slots for discussion, and checked whether any agenda items required special notice waivers.
keyTopics: Finalizing the June 16, 2026 council agenda with new items and time assignments
Planning the June 23, 2026 draft agenda
Community and Economic Development Committee Work Plan review

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 9, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 9, 2026, that included a private session to discuss hiring a new City Attorney, followed by a public session where the council approved proclamations recognizing Pride Month and Juneteenth.
keyTopics: City Attorney hiring decision (discussed in closed session)
Pride Month proclamation
Juneteenth proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 16, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on June 16, 2026, that included a private closed-door session followed by a public study session. The main topic discussed in the public portion was an update on the city's Community Equity Assessment.
keyTopics: Community Equity Assessment update and progress
Closed session
Study session format for community equity discussion

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review how parks and recreational facilities are currently zoned and designated in Cupertino, and to make sure local rules match California state law protections. The meeting also included a public comment period where residents could speak about topics not on the official agenda.
keyTopics: Review of zoning rules for parks and recreational facilities
Corrections to existing park and parkland designations
Ensuring local protections align with state law requirements
Public comment period on non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Stadium Neighborhood Relations Ad Hoc Subcommittee is holding a meeting on June 1, 2026 to discuss issues affecting neighborhoods around the stadium. Residents can attend in person or join remotely via Zoom, and can submit written comments by email.
keyTopics: Stadium neighborhood relations and community concerns
In-person and remote participation options for residents
Public comment submission process (email deadline: 10 a.m. day before meeting)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review updates to federal housing and community development funding plans for 2025-2026, consider changes to how council vacancies are filled, and handle routine administrative matters like approving meeting minutes and scheduling.
keyTopics: Updates to federal housing funding plan (Consolidated Plan) for 2025-2030
Proposed changes to council vacancy procedures
New workplan requests from individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 28, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss updates from various departments and committees, with major focus on improvements to the Foothills Nature Preserve and decisions about how to manage the middle school athletics program going forward. The council also reviewed the Parks and Recreation Commission's work plan for the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: Foothills Nature Preserve Improvements Project – funding and timeline for upgrades
Middle School Athletics Program – deciding on future management and program direction
Parks and Recreation Commission Work Plan for 2027 – budget and priorities
Department and Committee Updates – reports from city staff and liaison groups
